popularStory.headline: Arthur Howland 1602
popularStory.description: Arthur Howland, the oldest of three brothers came to New Plymouth about1623/4 with his brother Henry, where he lived a few years and then becamea landholder and resident of Marshfield, Massachusetts. …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry Howland II of Fenstanton.於大約1603年出生在英國亨廷格多史爾分斯坦頓時，他的父親Henry Howland Of Fenstanton40歲，母親Margaret38歲。他於1627年與Mary Unknown在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於1636年被列為became constable of duxbury。他去世於1671年1月1日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼度波利享年69歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼度波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Henry Howland II of Fenstanton.于大约1603年出生在英国亨廷格多史爾分斯坦頓时，他的父亲Henry Howland Of Fenstanton40岁，母亲Margaret38岁。他于1627年与Mary Unknown在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他于1636年被列为became constable of duxbury。他去世于1671年1月1日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼度波利享年69岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼度波利。

nameMeanings.name: Howland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Holland
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.description: In 1620, after 2 rough months at sea, 102 Pilgrims landed at Plymouth Rock and established one of the very first English settlements in America.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry Howland II of Fenstanton. was born about 1603, in Fenstanton, Huntingdonshire, England, his father, Henry Howland Of Fenstanton, was 40 and his mother, Margaret, was 38. He married Mary Unknown in 1627, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. In 1636, at the age of 34, his occupation is listed as became constable of duxbury. He died on 1 January 1671, in Duxbury, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 69, and was buried in Duxbury, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Henry Howland II of Fenstanton. est né environ 1603, à Fenstanton, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, son père, Henry Howland Of Fenstanton, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Margaret, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Mary Unknown en 1627, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. En 1636, à l’âge de 34 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est became constable of duxbury. Il est décédé le 1 janvier 1671, à Duxbury, Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterré à Duxbury, Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Henry Howland II of Fenstanton. um 1603 in Fenstanton, Huntingdonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry Howland Of Fenstanton 40 und seine Mutter Margaret 38. Er hat 1627 in England, United Kingdom Mary Unknown geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. 1636, als er 34 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als became constable of duxbury aufgeführt. Er starb am 1. Januar 1671 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Duxbury, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Duxbury, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Henry Howland II of Fenstanton. è nato il circa 1603 nel Fenstanton, Huntingdonshire, England, il suo padre, Henry Howland Of Fenstanton, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Margaret, aveva 38 anni. Lui sposato Mary Unknown nel 1627, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Nel 1636, all'età di 34 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come became constable of duxbury. Morì il 1 gennaio 1671, nel Duxbury, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolto a Duxbury, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry Howland II of Fenstanton.が約1603年にイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアフエンスタントンで生まれた時，父Henry Howland Of Fenstantonは40歳，母Margaretは38歳であった。1627年にイギリスイギリスでMary Unknownと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。1636年，34歳の時，職業はbecame constable of duxburyと記録されている。彼は1671年1月1日にイギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーダクスブリーで69歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーダクスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Henry Howland II of Fenstanton.(이)가 대략 1603년 영국 헌틴그도실르 펜스탠턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Howland Of Fenstanton(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret(은)는 38세였다. 그는 1627년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Mary Unknown(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 1636년, 34세의 나이로 그의 직업은 became constable of duxbury이었다. 그는 1671년 1월 1일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 드스버이에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 드스버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Henry Howland II of Fenstanton. nasceu aproximadamente 1603, em Fenstanton, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry Howland Of Fenstanton, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Margaret, tinha 38 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Unknown em 1627, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Em 1636, com 34 anos, sua profissão aparece como became constable of duxbury. Ele faleceu em 1 de janeiro de 1671, em Duxbury, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, com 69 anos, e foi sepultado em Duxbury, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry Howland II of Fenstanton. родился около 1603 г. в местности фенстантон, хантингдоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry Howland Of Fenstanton было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Unknown в 1627 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. В 1636 году, в возрасте 34 лет,  он работал по профессии became constable of duxbury. Он умер 1 января 1671 г. в местности дуксбури, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 69 лет и был похоронен в местности дуксбури, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Henry Howland II of Fenstanton. nació aproximadamente 1603, en Fenstanton, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry Howland Of Fenstanton, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Margaret, tenía 38 años. Se casó con Mary Unknown en 1627, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. En 1636, a la edad de 34 años, su ocupación registrada era became constable of duxbury. Murió el 1 de enero de 1671, en Duxbury, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultado en Duxbury, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.
